what_it_does: A grounding/confirmation result for the A2-canvas-mobile.html mockup (a mobile 'running chain' view). Holds 21 candidate UI-element dossiers reduced to 12 clusters, each with a ui:// address, capability tags, grounding flag, the what/what-you-can-do/how-to-change triple, a maps_to_feature id, and a 3-draw jury verdict sorting them into confirmed (4) and flagged (8).
observations: ground block frames the screen as a mobile live data-flow map with zoom and a bottom 'Run' button.
Confirmed items include the memo-gate node card (unanimous), the Run toolbar button (cluster_size 5), an instructions overlay, and the Canvas tabbar entry.
Refcheck fabrications flagged: CAN-brand and RUN-chain (maps_to_feature not an inventory id nor 'proposed').
Several proposed/built elements flagged purely on the soft jury with 0-of-3 grounded (brand, context, legend, chain, inbox tab).
Same schema as the desktop sibling: capabilities vocabulary, grounding built/proposed, two-layer judging (refcheck floor + accuracy jury).
tabbar entries split across canvas/inbox/rhm addresses each mapped to distinct features (CAN-compose, INB-surface, RHM-modes).
standouts: The RUN-run toolbar button absorbs 5 candidate indices into one confirmed dossier, showing heavy clustering of repeated detections.
RHM tabbar dossier 'represents' value is the literal glyph 'RHM ◈', carrying mockup iconography into the record.
conventions: Mirrors the canvas-desktop file's schema exactly (ground / counts / confirmed / flagged).
ui://<region>/<element> addressing; closed capability + grounding vocabularies.
howto triple on every dossier; verdict object with votes/unanimous/quorum.
concerns: Built-grounded elements (zoomhint, legend) flagged with 0-of-3 jury agreement — the 'built' claim and the jury disagree within the same dossier.
RHM tabbar maps to RHM-modes but its how-to describes a 'running chain' state, a description/feature mismatch internal to the dossier.
notes: The mobile-canvas slice of the same UI-grounding audit; fewer elements than desktop, same confirmed/flagged adjudication producing 4 confirmed and 8 flagged clusters.
questions: Why do several 'built'-grounded elements still receive 0-of-3 jury grounding — is the jury judging the description or the code link?
Is the mobile candidate set a subset/re-detection of the desktop one, or independently extracted?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: a per-element grounding audit of the mobile canvas mockup, as stated.
intention_signals: Aims to apply the same honest grounding audit to the mobile canvas variant, verifying that mobile element descriptions and feature claims are code-backed and flagging the doubtful ones.
novelty: Mobile-specific element set (tabbar navigation entries, bottom Run, legend) judged under the same verdict machinery as desktop.
contribution: Contributes the mobile-canvas grounding-audit record: the mobile element inventory, their feature mappings, and the confirmed/flagged verdicts a fused system would keep as the canvas-mobile grounding evidence.
summary_for_embedding: A UI-grounding audit result for the A2 canvas-mobile running-chain mockup, clustering 21 element dossiers into 4 confirmed and 8 flagged via refcheck and a 3-draw soft jury.
intention_for_embedding: To verify each mobile canvas element is truthfully grounded in real code and flag fabricated or unsupported feature mappings for operator review.
